== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2122005002222 == Commons category (P373) == * Shibukawa-jinja (Yao) == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市植松町三丁目3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:渋川神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11561593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shibukawa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Uematsu-chō 3-3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}}, {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|lt=26th|1=July 26}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shibukawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shibukawa Shrine''' (しぶかわじんじゃ) is a shrine in Uematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === * Enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hidaka Ōkami, and Sugawara Ōkami. * The date of foundation is unknown. It is also said to be the site of the Shibukawa residence of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, whose base was in this area in the Asuka period. * Originally it stood at Aza Kawamukai east of the old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but a great flood in Tenbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1533|Q6443|WD=Q6443|1=1533}}) washed away the entire precinct. At that time, Ukishima Shrine drifted to Nitta Village in Matta District, several kilometers north. Later, in Genki 2 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|WD=Q6670|1=1572}}), it was rebuilt at the current site, where the otabisho had been. * In the precinct stand several large trees, including the Osaka Prefecture natural monument {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. === Festivals === * Summer festival (July 25-26): commonly called the “reverse festival.” When the shrine was swept away by flood in the past, the sacred object reportedly flowed upstream against the current, giving rise to the name. === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === [[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), Kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine}}]] * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of cultural properties designated in Osaka Prefecture] from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website.. === Access === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yao Station|Q856485|WD=Q856485|1=Yao Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|WD=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}}. It is located just south of the station. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shibukawa temple ruins|Q109356865|WD=Q109356865|1=Shibugawa Haiji}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しふかわしんしや やおし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:物部守屋]] [[Category:龍華]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2122005002222 == Commons category (P373) == * Shibukawa-jinja (Yao) == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市植松町三丁目3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:渋川神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11561593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shibukawa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Uematsu-chō 3-3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}}, {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|lt=26th|1=July 26}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shibukawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shibukawa Shrine''' (しぶかわじんじゃ) is a shrine in Uematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === * Enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hidaka Ōkami, and Sugawara Ōkami. * The date of foundation is unknown. It is also said to be the site of the Shibukawa residence of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, whose base was in this area in the Asuka period. * Originally it stood at Aza Kawamukai east of the old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but a great flood in Tenbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1533|Q6443|WD=Q6443|1=1533}}) washed away the entire precinct. At that time, Ukishima Shrine drifted to Nitta Village in Matta District, several kilometers north. Later, in Genki 2 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|WD=Q6670|1=1572}}), it was rebuilt at the current site, where the otabisho had been. * In the precinct stand several large trees, including the Osaka Prefecture natural monument {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. === Festivals === * Summer festival (July 25-26): commonly called the “reverse festival.” When the shrine was swept away by flood in the past, the sacred object reportedly flowed upstream against the current, giving rise to the name. === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === [[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), Kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine}}]] * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of cultural properties designated in Osaka Prefecture] from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website.. === Access === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yao Station|Q856485|WD=Q856485|1=Yao Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|WD=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}}. It is located just south of the station. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shibukawa temple ruins|Q109356865|WD=Q109356865|1=Shibugawa Haiji}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:物部守屋]] [[Category:龍華]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2122005002222 == Commons category (P373) == * Shibukawa-jinja (Yao) == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市植松町三丁目3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:渋川神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11561593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shibukawa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Uematsu-chō 3-3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}}, {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|lt=26th|1=July 26}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shibukawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shibukawa Shrine''' (しぶかわじんじゃ) is a shrine in Uematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === * Enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hidaka Ōkami, and Sugawara Ōkami. * The date of foundation is unknown. It is also said to be the site of the Shibukawa residence of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, whose base was in this area in the Asuka period. * Originally it stood at Aza Kawamukai east of the old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but a great flood in Tenbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1533|Q6443|WD=Q6443|1=1533}}) washed away the entire precinct. At that time, Ukishima Shrine drifted to Nitta Village in Matta District, several kilometers north. Later, in Genki 2 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|WD=Q6670|1=1572}}), it was rebuilt at the current site, where the otabisho had been. * In the precinct stand several large trees, including the Osaka Prefecture natural monument {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. === Festivals === * Summer festival (July 25-26): commonly called the “reverse festival.” When the shrine was swept away by flood in the past, the sacred object reportedly flowed upstream against the current, giving rise to the name. === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === [[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), Kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine}}]] * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of cultural properties designated in Osaka Prefecture] from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website.. === Access === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yao Station|Q856485|WD=Q856485|1=Yao Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|WD=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}}. It is located just south of the station. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shibukawa temple ruins|Q109356865|WD=Q109356865|1=Shibugawa Haiji}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:物部守屋]] [[Category:龍華]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2122005002222 == Commons category (P373) == * Shibukawa-jinja (Yao) == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市植松町三丁目3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:渋川神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11561593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shibukawa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Uematsu-chō 3-3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}}, {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|lt=26th|1=July 26}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shibukawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shibukawa Shrine''' (しぶかわじんじゃ) is a shrine in Uematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === * Enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hidaka Ōkami, and Sugawara Ōkami. * The date of foundation is unknown. It is also said to be the site of the Shibukawa residence of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, whose base was in this area in the Asuka period. * Originally it stood at Aza Kawamukai east of the old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but a great flood in Tenbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1533|Q6443|WD=Q6443|1=1533}}) washed away the entire precinct. At that time, Ukishima Shrine drifted to Nitta Village in Matta District, several kilometers north. Later, in Genki 2 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|WD=Q6670|1=1572}}), it was rebuilt at the current site, where the otabisho had been. * In the precinct stand several large trees, including the Osaka Prefecture natural monument {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. === Festivals === * Summer festival (July 25-26): commonly called the “reverse festival.” When the shrine was swept away by flood in the past, the sacred object reportedly flowed upstream against the current, giving rise to the name. === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === [[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), Kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine}}]] * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of cultural properties designated in Osaka Prefecture] from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website.. === Access === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yao Station|Q856485|WD=Q856485|1=Yao Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|WD=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}}. It is located just south of the station. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shibukawa temple ruins|Q109356865|WD=Q109356865|1=Shibugawa Haiji}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:物部守屋]] [[Category:龍華]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2122005002222 == Commons category (P373) == * Shibukawa-jinja (Yao) == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市植松町三丁目3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:渋川神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11561593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shibukawa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Uematsu-chō 3-3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}}, {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|lt=26th|1=July 26}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shibukawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shibukawa Shrine''' (しぶかわじんじゃ) is a shrine in Uematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === * Enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hidaka Ōkami, and Sugawara Ōkami. * The date of foundation is unknown. It is also said to be the site of the Shibukawa residence of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, whose base was in this area in the Asuka period. * Originally it stood at Aza Kawamukai east of the old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but a great flood in Tenbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1533|Q6443|WD=Q6443|1=1533}}) washed away the entire precinct. At that time, Ukishima Shrine drifted to Nitta Village in Matta District, several kilometers north. Later, in Genki 2 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|WD=Q6670|1=1572}}), it was rebuilt at the current site, where the otabisho had been. * In the precinct stand several large trees, including the Osaka Prefecture natural monument {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. === Festivals === * Summer festival (July 25-26): commonly called the “reverse festival.” When the shrine was swept away by flood in the past, the sacred object reportedly flowed upstream against the current, giving rise to the name. === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === [[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), Kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine}}]] * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of cultural properties designated in Osaka Prefecture] from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website.. === Access === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yao Station|Q856485|WD=Q856485|1=Yao Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|WD=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}}. It is located just south of the station. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shibukawa temple ruins|Q109356865|WD=Q109356865|1=Shibugawa Haiji}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:物部守屋]] [[Category:龍華]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2122005002222 == Commons category (P373) == * Shibukawa-jinja (Yao) == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市植松町三丁目3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:渋川神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11561593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shibukawa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Uematsu-chō 3-3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}}, {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|lt=26th|1=July 26}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shibukawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shibukawa Shrine''' (しぶかわじんじゃ) is a shrine in Uematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === * Enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hidaka Ōkami, and Sugawara Ōkami. * The date of foundation is unknown. It is also said to be the site of the Shibukawa residence of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, whose base was in this area in the Asuka period. * Originally it stood at Aza Kawamukai east of the old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but a great flood in Tenbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1533|Q6443|WD=Q6443|1=1533}}) washed away the entire precinct. At that time, Ukishima Shrine drifted to Nitta Village in Matta District, several kilometers north. Later, in Genki 2 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|WD=Q6670|1=1572}}), it was rebuilt at the current site, where the otabisho had been. * In the precinct stand several large trees, including the Osaka Prefecture natural monument {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. === Festivals === * Summer festival (July 25-26): commonly called the “reverse festival.” When the shrine was swept away by flood in the past, the sacred object reportedly flowed upstream against the current, giving rise to the name. === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === [[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), Kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine}}]] * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of cultural properties designated in Osaka Prefecture] from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website.. === Access === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yao Station|Q856485|WD=Q856485|1=Yao Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|WD=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}}. It is located just south of the station. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shibukawa temple ruins|Q109356865|WD=Q109356865|1=Shibugawa Haiji}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:物部守屋]] [[Category:龍華]][[Category:qq]]

seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市植松町三丁目3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:渋川神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11561593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shibukawa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Uematsu-chō 3-3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}}, {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|lt=26th|1=July 26}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shibukawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shibukawa Shrine''' (しぶかわじんじゃ) is a shrine in Uematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === * Enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hidaka Ōkami, and Sugawara Ōkami. * The date of foundation is unknown. It is also said to be the site of the Shibukawa residence of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, whose base was in this area in the Asuka period. * Originally it stood at Aza Kawamukai east of the old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but a great flood in Tenbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1533|Q6443|WD=Q6443|1=1533}}) washed away the entire precinct. At that time, Ukishima Shrine drifted to Nitta Village in Matta District, several kilometers north. Later, in Genki 2 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|WD=Q6670|1=1572}}), it was rebuilt at the current site, where the otabisho had been. * In the precinct stand several large trees, including the Osaka Prefecture natural monument {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. === Festivals === * Summer festival (July 25-26): commonly called the “reverse festival.” When the shrine was swept away by flood in the past, the sacred object reportedly flowed upstream against the current, giving rise to the name. === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === [[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), Kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine}}]] * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of cultural properties designated in Osaka Prefecture] from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website.. === Access === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yao Station|Q856485|WD=Q856485|1=Yao Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|WD=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}}. It is located just south of the station. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shibukawa temple ruins|Q109356865|WD=Q109356865|1=Shibugawa Haiji}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:物部守屋]] [[Category:龍華]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2122005002222 == Commons category (P373) == * Shibukawa-jinja (Yao) == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市植松町三丁目3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:渋川神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11561593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shibukawa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Uematsu-chō 3-3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}}, {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|lt=26th|1=July 26}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shibukawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shibukawa Shrine''' (しぶかわじんじゃ) is a shrine in Uematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === * Enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hidaka Ōkami, and Sugawara Ōkami. * The date of foundation is unknown. It is also said to be the site of the Shibukawa residence of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, whose base was in this area in the Asuka period. * Originally it stood at Aza Kawamukai east of the old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but a great flood in Tenbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1533|Q6443|WD=Q6443|1=1533}}) washed away the entire precinct. At that time, Ukishima Shrine drifted to Nitta Village in Matta District, several kilometers north. Later, in Genki 2 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|WD=Q6670|1=1572}}), it was rebuilt at the current site, where the otabisho had been. * In the precinct stand several large trees, including the Osaka Prefecture natural monument {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. === Festivals === * Summer festival (July 25-26): commonly called the “reverse festival.” When the shrine was swept away by flood in the past, the sacred object reportedly flowed upstream against the current, giving rise to the name. === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === [[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), Kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine}}]] * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of cultural properties designated in Osaka Prefecture] from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website.. === Access === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yao Station|Q856485|WD=Q856485|1=Yao Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|WD=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}}. It is located just south of the station. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shibukawa temple ruins|Q109356865|WD=Q109356865|1=Shibugawa Haiji}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe no Moriya]] [[Category:龍華]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2122005002222 == Commons category (P373) == * Shibukawa-jinja (Yao) == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市植松町三丁目3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:渋川神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11561593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shibukawa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Uematsu-chō 3-3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}}, {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|lt=26th|1=July 26}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shibukawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shibukawa Shrine''' (しぶかわじんじゃ) is a shrine in Uematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === * Enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hidaka Ōkami, and Sugawara Ōkami. * The date of foundation is unknown. It is also said to be the site of the Shibukawa residence of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, whose base was in this area in the Asuka period. * Originally it stood at Aza Kawamukai east of the old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but a great flood in Tenbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1533|Q6443|WD=Q6443|1=1533}}) washed away the entire precinct. At that time, Ukishima Shrine drifted to Nitta Village in Matta District, several kilometers north. Later, in Genki 2 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|WD=Q6670|1=1572}}), it was rebuilt at the current site, where the otabisho had been. * In the precinct stand several large trees, including the Osaka Prefecture natural monument {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. === Festivals === * Summer festival (July 25-26): commonly called the “reverse festival.” When the shrine was swept away by flood in the past, the sacred object reportedly flowed upstream against the current, giving rise to the name. === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === [[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), Kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine}}]] * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of cultural properties designated in Osaka Prefecture] from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website.. === Access === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yao Station|Q856485|WD=Q856485|1=Yao Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|WD=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}}. It is located just south of the station. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shibukawa temple ruins|Q109356865|WD=Q109356865|1=Shibugawa Haiji}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe no Moriya]] [[Category:龍華]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2122005002222 == Commons category (P373) == * Shibukawa-jinja (Yao) == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市植松町三丁目3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:渋川神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11561593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shibukawa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Uematsu-chō 3-3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}}, {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|lt=26th|1=July 26}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shibukawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shibukawa Shrine''' (しぶかわじんじゃ) is a shrine in Uematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === * Enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hidaka Ōkami, and Sugawara Ōkami. * The date of foundation is unknown. It is also said to be the site of the Shibukawa residence of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, whose base was in this area in the Asuka period. * Originally it stood at Aza Kawamukai east of the old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but a great flood in Tenbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1533|Q6443|WD=Q6443|1=1533}}) washed away the entire precinct. At that time, Ukishima Shrine drifted to Nitta Village in Matta District, several kilometers north. Later, in Genki 2 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|WD=Q6670|1=1572}}), it was rebuilt at the current site, where the otabisho had been. * In the precinct stand several large trees, including the Osaka Prefecture natural monument {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. === Festivals === * Summer festival (July 25-26): commonly called the “reverse festival.” When the shrine was swept away by flood in the past, the sacred object reportedly flowed upstream against the current, giving rise to the name. === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === [[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), Kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine}}]] * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of cultural properties designated in Osaka Prefecture] from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website.. === Access === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yao Station|Q856485|WD=Q856485|1=Yao Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|WD=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}}. It is located just south of the station. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shibukawa temple ruins|Q109356865|WD=Q109356865|1=Shibugawa Haiji}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe no Moriya]] [[Category:龍華]]

seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市植松町三丁目3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:渋川神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11561593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shibukawa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Uematsu-chō 3-3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}}, {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|lt=26th|1=July 26}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shibukawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shibukawa Shrine''' (しぶかわじんじゃ) is a shrine in Uematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === * Enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hidaka Ōkami, and Sugawara Ōkami. * The date of foundation is unknown. It is also said to be the site of the Shibukawa residence of {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, whose base was in this area in the Asuka period. * Originally it stood at Aza Kawamukai east of the old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}, but a great flood in Tenbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1533|Q6443|WD=Q6443|1=1533}}) washed away the entire precinct. At that time, Ukishima Shrine drifted to Nitta Village in Matta District, several kilometers north. Later, in Genki 2 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|WD=Q6670|1=1572}}), it was rebuilt at the current site, where the otabisho had been. * In the precinct stand several large trees, including the Osaka Prefecture natural monument {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. === Festivals === * Summer festival (July 25-26): commonly called the “reverse festival.” When the shrine was swept away by flood in the past, the sacred object reportedly flowed upstream against the current, giving rise to the name. === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === [[File:Shibukawa-jinja (Yao), Kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine}}]] * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Shibukawa Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of cultural properties designated in Osaka Prefecture] from Yao City file on Osaka Prefecture website.. === Access === * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yao Station|Q856485|WD=Q856485|1=Yao Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|WD=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}}. It is located just south of the station. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shibukawa temple ruins|Q109356865|WD=Q109356865|1=Shibugawa Haiji}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Shibukawa-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shibukawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe no Moriya]] [[Category:龍華]]
